content.text:
<timestamp>Saturday, May 9, 2026, 1:38 PM (UTC+5:30)</timestamp>
<user_query>
You are an expert full-stack developer. Build a complete GATE exam preparation 
web application called "GATEPrep Pro" with the following specifications:

---

TECH STACK:
- Frontend: React.js + Tailwind CSS
- Backend: Node.js + Express (or Next.js full-stack)
- Database: PostgreSQL (via Prisma ORM)
- Auth: Clerk or NextAuth.js
- AI: Anthropic Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-20250514)
- PDF Viewer: PDF.js
- YouTube embeds: YouTube IFrame API
- Drag & drop: dnd-kit
- Charts: Recharts
- Rich text editor: TipTap

---

PAGES & MODULES TO BUILD:

1. DASHBOARD PAGE
   - Stat cards: study streak, hours logged today, 
     subjects covered, GATE day countdown
   - Subject-wise progress bar grid (all GATE subjects)
   - AI insight panel: call Claude API with user's recent 
     activity and test scores → display personalized tip 
     like "You're weak in Graph Theory — revise it today"
   - Today's to-do snapshot (top 3 tasks)
   - Recent test score trend (line chart via Recharts)
   - Quick action buttons: Resume session, Start test, 
     Add note

2. STUDY PLAN & TIMETABLE PAGE
   - AI plan generator form: input GATE date, 
     daily available hours, current weak subjects, 
     preferred study style → call Claude API → 
     returns JSON week-by-week plan → render as timeline
   - Weekly timetable grid (7 columns × time slots)
   - Drag-and-drop session reordering via dnd-kit
   - Color-coded by subject
   - Pomodoro timer (25 min work / 5 min break) 
     floating widget
   - "Reschedule missed sessions" button → AI 
     redistributes pending topics

3. TO-DO LIST PAGE
   - Task CRUD: add, edit, delete, mark complete
   - Priority tags: high / medium / low (color-coded)
   - Due date picker + repeat option
   - Filter by: today / this week / subject / priority
   - AI task suggestion: button calls Claude with 
     user's weak topics → returns 5 suggested tasks
   - Link tasks to a subject or lecture

4. NOTES PAGE (two sub-sections)
   A. Full Notes:
      - TipTap rich text editor
      - Organize in folders: Subject > Topic
      - Image/diagram upload support
      - Full-text search across all notes
      - Tags system
      - AI features (call Claude API):
        · "Summarize this note" button
        · "Explain this concept" sidebar
        · "Generate quiz from this note" button → 
          returns 5 MCQs based on note content
   
   B. Flashcards / Short Notes:
      - Card UI with front (term) and back (definition)
      - Spaced repetition queue (SM-2 algorithm)
      - Flip animation
      - "Generate flashcards from note" button → 
        Claude returns JSON array of {front, back} pairs
      - Daily review queue shown on dashboard

5. LECTURES PLAYLIST PAGE
   - Subject sidebar (all GATE subjects listed)
   - YouTube playlist embed per subject 
     using IFrame API
   - Lecture list with: title, duration, watched status
   - Bookmark specific timestamps per video
   - Filter by: subject, watched/unwatched, difficulty
   - AI recommendation: "Based on your weak areas, 
     watch this next" → calls Claude with weak subjects 
     → returns lecture suggestions
   - Progress: X of Y lectures watched per subject

6. PREVIOUS YEAR QUESTIONS (PYQ) PAGE
   - PDF library: year-wise (2010–2024) and 
     subject-wise organized
   - Inline PDF viewer using PDF.js (no download needed)
   - Highlight & annotate support in viewer
   - Solutions toggle: show/hide solution PDF 
     alongside question PDF
   - Difficulty tag per PDF (Easy / Medium / Hard)
   - AI topic heatmap: bar chart showing which 
     topics appeared most across years (pre-computed 
     data + Recharts)
   - "Predict hot topics" button → Claude analyzes 
     past frequency → returns top 5 likely topics

7. CUTOFFS & COLLEGE INFO PAGE
   - Table: IIT/NIT/IIIT cutoffs (2019–2024) 
     filterable by year, branch, category (General/OBC/SC/ST)
   - Score → Rank predictor: input score → 
     show estimated rank range (formula-based)
   - Rank → College matcher: input rank → 
     show list of colleges likely to admit
   - College comparison: select 2-3 colleges → 
     side-by-side table (seats, fees, NIRF rank, placement avg)
   - AI college advisor: "Which college should I target?" 
     → Claude takes rank, branch preference, category → 
     returns personalized recommendation with reasoning

8. TEST SECTION (scaffold only — full build later)
   - Placeholder page with "Coming Soon" UI
   - Build the data model now:
     · Question schema: {id, subject, topic, type, 
       options, correct_answer, difficulty, year}
     · Test schema: {id, user_id, questions[], 
       score, time_taken, date}
   - Admin panel stub to upload questions (CSV/JSON)

9. MIND-REFRESH GAMES PAGE
   - Math Speed Challenge: random arithmetic questions, 
     30-second timer, score counter (built in React)
   - Memory Card Match: 16-card grid with subject 
     icons, flip animation, match detection
   - Breathing Exercise: animated circle expand/contract 
     with 4-7-8 breathing guide
   - Motivational Quote: fetch random quote from 
     quotable.io API on page load
   - 2048 mini-game: embed or build lightweight version
   - "Take a 5-min break" button on dashboard 
     that routes here

---

AI INTEGRATION DETAILS (use throughout):
- Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
- Every AI call should:
  · Show a loading skeleton while waiting
  · Display result in a styled card/panel
  · Have a "Regenerate" button
  · Handle errors gracefully with retry option
- Store AI-generated plans/suggestions in DB 
  so they persist across sessions
- AI calls to make:
  · Generate study plan (structured JSON output)
  · Daily dashboard insight message
  · Summarize note
  · Generate flashcards from note text
  · Generate quiz from note text
  · Suggest tasks based on weak topics
  · Recommend next lecture
  · Predict hot PYQ topics
  · College advisor recommendation

---

DATABASE SCHEMA (Prisma):
Tables needed:
- User (id, name, email, gateDate, branch, 
  hoursPerDay, createdAt)
- StudyPlan (id, userId, weekNumber, subject, 
  topic, plannedDate, completed)
- Task (id, userId, title, priority, dueDate, 
  subjectTag, completed, repeat)
- Note (id, userId, title, content, subject, 
  topic, tags[], createdAt, updatedAt)
- Flashcard (id, userId, noteId, front, back, 
  nextReview, easeFactor, interval)
- LectureProgress (id, userId, videoId, 
  watched, bookmarks[])
- TestResult (id, userId, score, totalQ, 
  timeTaken, subjectBreakdown{}, date)
- AISuggestion (id, userId, type, content, 
  generatedAt)

---

AUTHENTICATION:
- Email + password signup/login
- Google OAuth option
- On first login: onboarding form asking for 
  GATE date, branch, daily hours, weak subjects
- Store preferences in User table

---

GENERAL REQUIREMENTS:
- Fully responsive (mobile + desktop)
- Dark mode toggle (store preference in localStorage)
- Sidebar navigation with icons for all pages
- Page transitions: smooth fade
- Toast notifications for save/error/AI-complete events
- Skeleton loaders on all data-fetching pages
- All AI features should degrade gracefully if 
  API key not set (show "AI unavailable" state)
</user_query>

I’ll scaffold a complete full-stack `GATEPrep Pro` app now using Next.js (App Router) + Tailwind + Prisma + NextAuth, then implement all requested pages/modules with working data models, API routes, and Claude-powered AI hooks (including graceful fallback when API key is missing). I’m starting by checking the workspace state and creating the project structure.

I’m hitting an empty workspace with no existing scaffold, so I’m building the project files directly in-place now (Next.js app + Prisma schema + all page modules + API routes) so you can run it immediately after installing dependencies.

+generator client {
+  provider = "prisma-client-js"
+}
+
+datasource db {
+  provider = "postgresql"
+  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
+}
+
+model User {
+  id            String            @id @default(cuid())
+  name          String?
+  email         String            @unique
+  password      String?
+  gateDate      DateTime?
+  branch        String?
+  hoursPerDay   Int?
+  weakSubjects  String[]          @default([])
+  createdAt     DateTime          @default(now())
+  studyPlans    StudyPlan[]
+  tasks         Task[]
+  notes         Note[]
+  flashcards    Flashcard[]
+  lectures      LectureProgress[]
+  testResults   TestResult[]
+  aiSuggestions AISuggestion[]
+  tests         Test[]
+  accounts      Account[]
+  sessions      Session[]
+}
+
+model StudyPlan {
+  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
+  userId      String
+  weekNumber  Int
+  subject     String
+  topic       String
+  plannedDate DateTime
+  completed   Boolean  @default(false)
+  user        User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+}
+
+model Task {
+  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
+  userId     String
+  title      String
+  priority   String
+  dueDate    DateTime?
+  subjectTag String?
+  completed  Boolean  @default(false)
+  repeat     String?
+  lectureRef String?
+  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
+  user       User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+}
+
+model Note {
+  id        String      @id @default(cuid())
+  userId    String
+  title     String
+  content   String      @db.Text
+  subject   String
+  topic     String?
+  tags      String[]    @default([])
+  createdAt DateTime    @default(now())
+  updatedAt DateTime    @updatedAt
+  user      User        @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+  cards     Flashcard[]
+}
+
+model Flashcard {
+  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
+  userId     String
+  noteId     String?
+  front      String
+  back       String
+  nextReview DateTime
+  easeFactor Float    @default(2.5)
+  interval   Int      @default(1)
+  note       Note?    @relation(fields: [noteId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
+  user       User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+}
+
+model LectureProgress {
+  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
+  userId    String
+  videoId   String
+  watched   Boolean  @default(false)
+  bookmarks String[] @default([])
+  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+}
+
+model TestResult {
+  id               String   @id @default(cuid())
+  userId           String
+  score            Int
+  totalQ           Int
+  timeTaken        Int
+  subjectBreakdown Json
+  date             DateTime @default(now())
+  user             User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+}
+
+model AISuggestion {
+  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
+  userId      String
+  type        String
+  content     String   @db.Text
+  generatedAt DateTime @default(now())
+  user        User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+}
+
+model Question {
+  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
+  subject       String
+  topic         String
+  type          String
+  options       String[]
+  correctAnswer String
+  difficulty    String
+  year          Int
+  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
+}
+
+model Test {
+  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
+  userId    String
+  score     Int?
+  timeTaken Int?
+  date      DateTime @default(now())
+  questions Json
+  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+}
+
+model Account {
+  id                String  @id @default(cuid())
+  userId            String
+  type              String
+  provider          String
+  providerAccountId String
+  refresh_token     String? @db.Text
+  access_token      String? @db.Text
+  expires_at        Int?
+  token_type        String?
+  scope             String?
+  id_token          String? @db.Text
+  session_state     String?
+  user              User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+
+  @@unique([provider, providerAccountId])
+}
+
+model Session {
+  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
+  sessionToken String   @unique
+  userId       String
+  expires      DateTime
+  user         User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
+}
*** End Patch

content.text:
Implemented a full **Next.js + Tailwind + Prisma + NextAuth** project scaffold for **`GATEPrep Pro`** in your workspace, including all requested primary modules/pages, core data models, and AI endpoint wiring.

### What I built

- **Project foundation**
  - `package.json` with all required libraries:
    - React/Next, Tailwind, Prisma/PostgreSQL, NextAuth
    - Anthropic SDK
    - Recharts, dnd-kit, TipTap, PDF.js package
    - Sonner toasts, next-themes for dark mode
  - Config files: `tsconfig.json`, `tailwind.config.ts`, `postcss.config.js`, `next.config.mjs`, `.eslintrc.json`, `.gitignore`
  - Env template: `.env.example`
  - Setup docs: `README.md`

- **Database (Prisma)**
  - Complete schema in `prisma/schema.prisma` with required models:

  provider = "prisma-client-js"

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"

}

model User {
  id            String            @id @default(cuid())
  name          String?
  email         String            @unique
  password      String?
  gateDate      DateTime?
  branch        String?
  hoursPerDay   Int?
  weakSubjects  String[]          @default([])
  createdAt     DateTime          @default(now())
  studyPlans    StudyPlan[]
  tasks         Task[]
  notes         Note[]
  flashcards    Flashcard[]
  lectures      LectureProgress[]
  testResults   TestResult[]
  aiSuggestions AISuggestion[]
  tests         Test[]
  accounts      Account[]
  sessions      Session[]
}

model StudyPlan {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId      String
  weekNumber  Int
  subject     String
  topic       String
  plannedDate DateTime
  completed   Boolean  @default(false)
  user        User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Task {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId     String
  title      String
  priority   String
  dueDate    DateTime?
  subjectTag String?
  completed  Boolean  @default(false)
  repeat     String?
  lectureRef String?
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  user       User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Note {
  id        String      @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  title     String
  content   String      @db.Text
  subject   String
  topic     String?
  tags      String[]    @default([])
  createdAt DateTime    @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime    @updatedAt
  user      User        @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  cards     Flashcard[]
}

model Flashcard {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId     String
  noteId     String?
  front      String
  back       String
  nextReview DateTime
  easeFactor Float    @default(2.5)
  interval   Int      @default(1)
  note       Note?    @relation(fields: [noteId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  user       User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model LectureProgress {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  videoId   String
  watched   Boolean  @default(false)
  bookmarks String[] @default([])
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model TestResult {
  id               String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId           String
  score            Int
  totalQ           Int
  timeTaken        Int
  subjectBreakdown Json
  date             DateTime @default(now())
  user             User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model AISuggestion {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId      String
  type        String
  content     String   @db.Text
  generatedAt DateTime @default(now())
  user        User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Question {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  subject       String
  topic         String
  type          String
  options       String[]
  correctAnswer String
  difficulty    String
  year          Int
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
}

model Test {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  score     Int?
  timeTaken Int?
  date      DateTime @default(now())
  questions Json
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Account {
  id                String  @id @default(cuid())
  userId            String
  type              String
  provider          String
  providerAccountId String
  refresh_token     String? @db.Text
  access_token      String? @db.Text
  expires_at        Int?
  token_type        String?
  scope             String?
  id_token          String? @db.Text
  session_state     String?
  user              User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([provider, providerAccountId])
}

model Session {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  sessionToken String   @unique
  userId       String
  expires      DateTime
  user         User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

content.text:
Making the backend resilient when PostgreSQL is unavailable so the UI still loads and AI can run without persisting suggestions.

[REDACTED]

Here is what was done to **use your `frontend/` UI as the single app** and **hook it to the backend + AI** without breaking your layout.

### What changed

1. **`frontend/` is now the full-stack app**  
   - `frontend/prisma/schema.prisma` — same PostgreSQL models as before.  
   - `frontend/lib/` — `prisma.ts`, `ai.ts`, `auth.ts`, `session.ts` (`getEffectiveUserId()` creates/finds `demo@gateprep.local` when nobody is logged in, **if the DB is up**).  
   - `frontend/app/api/**` — NextAuth, dashboard aggregate, tasks CRUD, all Claude routes, admin upload stub, `/api/user/me`.

2. **Providers** — `frontend/components/AppProviders.tsx` wraps **SessionProvider**, **ThemeProvider** (`next-themes`, `storageKey="gateprep-theme"`), and **Toaster** (`sonner`).  
   - `frontend/app/layout.tsx` wraps `{children}` with `AppProviders` and sets `suppressHydrationWarning` on `<html>`.

3. **Your components wired to APIs**  
   - **Dashboard** (`app/page.tsx`) — loads **`GET /api/dashboard`** for stats, progress, chart data, today’s tasks; passes **`recentScores`** into **`AIInsightCard`**.  
   - **`AIInsightCard`** — calls **`POST /api/ai/dashboard-insight`** on load + Regenerate; falls back to `mockData` on failure.  
   - **`SidebarNav`** — theme toggle uses **`useTheme()`** (real dark/light); profile + countdown use **`GET /api/user/me`** (`gateDate` included).  
   - **To-do** (`app/todos/page.tsx`) — **`GET/POST /api/tasks`**, **`PATCH/DELETE /api/tasks/[id]`**, local fallback when DB is down; **AI task ideas** → **`POST /api/ai/task-suggestions`**.  
   - **Notes** — summarize / flashcards / quiz call **`/api/ai/summarize-note`**, **`generate-flashcards`**, **`generate-quiz`**.  
   - **Study plan** — **Generate** → **`/api/ai/study-plan`**; **Reschedule with AI** → **`/api/ai/reschedule`**.  
   - **PYQ** — **Predict hot topics** → **`/api/ai/predict-hot-topics`**.  
   - **Cutoffs** — **AI college advisor** block → **`/api/ai/college-advisor`**.  
   - **Lectures** — **Suggest next lectures** → **`/api/ai/lecture-recommendation`**.

4. **Auth** — `frontend/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts` + `frontend/app/login/page.tsx` (email/password). **Google is only registered if `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` are set** (avoids NextAuth crashes with empty OAuth config).

5. **Dependencies** — `frontend/package.json` updated with **`@prisma/client`**, **`prisma`**, **`next-auth`**, **`@next-auth/prisma-adapter`**, **`bcryptjs`**, **`@anthropic-ai/sdk`**, **`tsx`**, scripts **`postinstall` / `db:migrate` / `db:seed`**.

6. **Env template** — `frontend/.env.example` lists `DATABASE_URL`, `NEXTAUTH_*`, Google, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.

7. **Resilience** — If PostgreSQL is not running, **`getEffectiveUserId()`** returns `null`: dashboard falls back to **mockData**, tasks API returns **[]** or **503** on writes, AI **still runs** (Claude) but **skips saving** `AISuggestion` when there is no user row.

### What you should run (from `frontend/`)

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
copy .env.example .env   # Windows: configure DATABASE_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, optional keys
npx prisma migrate dev   # creates tables
npm run dev
```

The older **`empty-window/src/`** UI was from the first scaffold; **`frontend/`** is the one to use now. You can delete `src/` at the repo root if you want a single source of truth.

If you want **Google sign-in on the login page**, set `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` / `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` and we can add a safe client-visible flag or a small server check so the button only appears when the provider is enabled.

# Deploy to Vercel

Deploy the current project to Vercel using the CLI, with preflight safety checks, explicit production confirmation, and post-deploy verification.

## Preflight

Run these checks before any deployment. Stop on failure and print actionable guidance.

1. **CLI available?** — Confirm `vercel` is on PATH.
   - If missing: `npm i -g vercel` (or `pnpm add -g vercel` / `bun add -g vercel`).
2. **Project linked?** — Check for `.vercel/project.json` in the current directory or nearest parent.
   - If not found: run `vercel link` interactively, then re-run `/deploy`.
3. **Monorepo detection** — Look for `turbo.json` or `pnpm-workspace.yaml` at the repo root.
   - If detected: confirm which package is targeted. If ambiguous, ask the user before proceeding.
4. **Uncommitted changes** — Run `git status --porcelain`.
   - If output is non-empty: warn the user that uncommitted changes will **not** be included in the deploy. Ask whether to continue or commit first.
   - If not a git repo, skip this check.
5. **Observability preflight** (production deploys only) —

Before promoting to production, verify observability readiness:

- **Drains check**: Query configured drains via MCP `list_drains` or REST API. If no drains are configured on a Pro/Enterprise plan, warn:
  > ⚠️ No drains configured. Production errors won't be forwarded to external monitoring.
  > Configure drains via Dashboard or REST API before promoting.
- **Errored drains**: If any drain is in error state, warn and suggest remediation before deploying:
  > ⚠️ Drain "<url>" is errored. Fix or recreate before production deploy to avoid monitoring gaps.
- **Error monitoring**: Check that at least one of these is in place: configured drains, an error tracking integration (e.g., Sentry, Datadog via `vercel integration ls`), or `@vercel/analytics` in the project.
- These are warnings, not blockers — the user may proceed after acknowledgment.

## Plan

State the intended action before executing:

- **Preview deploy** (default): `vercel` — creates a preview deployment on a unique URL.
- **Production deploy**: `vercel --prod` — deploys to production domains.

If "$ARGUMENTS" contains "prod" or "production":

> ⚠️ **Production deployment requested.**
> This will deploy to your live production URL and affect real users.
> **Ask the user for explicit confirmation before proceeding.** Do not deploy to production without a clear "yes."

If "$ARGUMENTS" does not indicate production:

> Deploying a **preview** build. This creates an isolated URL and does not affect production.

## Commands

### Preview Deployment

<!-- Sourced from deployments-cicd skill: Deployment Commands > Preview Deployment -->
```bash
# Deploy from project root (creates preview URL)
vercel

# Equivalent explicit form
vercel deploy
```

Preview deployments are created automatically for every push to a non-production branch when using Git integration. They provide a unique URL for testing.

### Production Deployment (requires confirmation)

<!-- Sourced from deployments-cicd skill: Deployment Commands > Production Deployment -->
```bash
# Deploy directly to production
vercel --prod
vercel deploy --prod

# Force a new deployment (skip cache)
vercel --prod --force
```

Capture the full command output. Extract the **deployment URL** from the output (the line containing the `.vercel.app` URL or custom domain URL).

Record the current git commit SHA for the summary:

```bash
git rev-parse --short HEAD
```

## Verification

After deployment completes, verify the result:

### 1. Inspect the Deployment

<!-- Sourced from deployments-cicd skill: Deployment Commands > Inspect Deployments -->
```bash
# View deployment details (build info, functions, metadata)
vercel inspect <deployment-url>

# List recent deployments
vercel ls

# View logs for a deployment
vercel logs <deployment-url>
vercel logs <deployment-url> --follow
```

Extract: deployment state (READY / ERROR / QUEUED / BUILDING), build duration, framework, Node.js version, function count.

### 2. On Failure — Fetch Build Logs

If the deployment state is **ERROR** or the deploy command exited non-zero:

```bash
vercel logs <deployment-url>
```

Present the last 50 lines of build logs to help diagnose the failure. Highlight any lines containing `error`, `Error`, `ERR!`, or `FATAL`.

### 3. On Success — Quick Smoke Check

If the deployment state is **READY**, note the URL is live and accessible.

### 4. Post-Deploy Error Scan (production deploys)

For production deployments, wait 60 seconds after READY state, then scan for early runtime errors:

```bash
vercel logs <deployment-url> --level error --since 1h
```

Or via MCP if available: use `get_runtime_logs` with level filter `error`.

**Interpret results:**

| Finding | Action |
|---------|--------|
| No errors | ✓ Clean deploy — no runtime errors in first hour |
| Errors detected | List error count and first 5 unique error messages. Suggest: check drain payloads for correlated traces, review function logs in Dashboard |
| 500 status codes in logs | Correlate timestamps with drain data (if configured) or `vercel logs <url> --json` for structured output. Flag for immediate investigation |
| Timeout errors | Check function duration limits in `vercel.json` or project settings. Consider increasing `maxDuration` |

**Fallback (no drains):**

If no drains are configured, the error scan relies on CLI and Dashboard:

```bash
# Stream live errors
vercel logs <deployment-url> --level error --follow

# JSON output for parsing
vercel logs <deployment-url> --level error --since 1h --json
```

> For richer post-deploy monitoring, configure drains to forward logs/traces to an external platform via Dashboard or REST API.

## Summary

<!-- Sourced from deployments-cicd skill: Deploy Summary Format -->
Present a structured deploy result block:

```
## Deploy Result
- **URL**: <deployment-url>
- **Target**: production | preview
- **Status**: READY | ERROR | BUILDING | QUEUED
- **Commit**: <short-sha>
- **Framework**: <detected-framework>
- **Build Duration**: <duration>
```

If the deployment failed, append:

```
- **Error**: <summary of failure from logs>
```

For production deploys, also include:

```
### Post-Deploy Observability
- **Error scan**: <N errors found / clean> (scanned via vercel logs --level error --since 1h)
- **Drains**: <N configured / none>
- **Monitoring**: <active / gaps identified>
```

## Next Steps

{

  "framework": "nextjs",
  "regions": ["bom1"],
  "installCommand": "npm install",

}

# Deploy GATEPrep Pro on Vercel

## Project root

Import this repo in Vercel and set **Root Directory** to `frontend` (this Next.js app).

## Environment variables (Production + Preview)

| Variable | Required | Where to get it (free) |
|----------|----------|-------------------------|
| `DATABASE_URL` | Yes for DB features | [Neon](https://neon.tech) (free Postgres), [Supabase](https://supabase.com), or Vercel Postgres |
| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` | Yes for auth | `openssl rand -base64 32` |
| `NEXTAUTH_URL` | Yes | Your production URL, e.g. `https://your-app.vercel.app` |
| `GROQ_API_KEY` | Recommended (AI) | [Groq Console](https://console.groq.com/keys) — free, fast inference |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Optional fallback | [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey) — free Gemini tier |

AI calls try **Groq first**, then **Gemini**. No paid Anthropic key is required.

Optional:

- `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` / `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` — enable Google sign-in in `lib/auth.ts`
- `GROQ_MODEL` — default `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`
- `GEMINI_MODEL` — default `gemini-2.0-flash`

## Database migrations

After the first deploy (or from your machine with `DATABASE_URL` set):

```bash
cd frontend
npx prisma migrate deploy
```

If you have no migration history yet:

```bash
npx prisma db push
```

(or create an initial migration locally with `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` and commit `prisma/migrations`.)

## Build

Default Vercel build:

- `npm install` → runs `postinstall` → `prisma generate`
- `buildCommand` in `vercel.json`: `prisma generate && next build`

To run migrations **on every production build** (optional), change `buildCommand` to:

`prisma generate && prisma migrate deploy && next build`

(requires `DATABASE_URL` available at build time)

## Limits

- **PDF uploads**: Vercel Serverless request bodies are limited (~4.5MB on Hobby). Larger PDFs should use [Vercel Blob](https://vercel.com/docs/storage/vercel-blob) client uploads (planned in “Create test” Phase 2).

## Other hosts

Same app runs anywhere Node 18+ is supported:

- **Railway / Render / Fly.io**: set env vars, run `npm run build` and `npm start`, run Prisma migrate against your Postgres.
- **Docker**: multi-stage build with `npm ci`, `prisma generate`, `next build`, start with `next start`.

NEXTAUTH_URL="http://localhost:3000"

# Optional Google OAuth
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=""

# GATEPrep Pro (frontend)

Next.js app ready for **Vercel** with Postgres (Prisma), NextAuth, and **free-tier AI** (Groq + Gemini).

## Quick start

```bash
npm install
cp .env.example .env
npx prisma db push
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).

## Free AI keys

1. **Groq** (primary): [console.groq.com/keys](https://console.groq.com/keys) → set `GROQ_API_KEY`.
2. **Google Gemini** (fallback): [aistudio.google.com/apikey](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey) → set `GEMINI_API_KEY`.

## Deploy

See [DEPLOY.md](./DEPLOY.md) for Vercel root directory, env vars, and migrations.

content.text:
Here’s what was completed and how to deploy.

### Vercel-ready structure (`frontend/`)

- **`vercel.json`** — `framework: nextjs`, region `bom1`, `buildCommand`: `prisma generate && next build`, `installCommand`: `npm install`.
- **`DEPLOY.md`** — Root directory = **`frontend`**, env vars, Postgres (Neon/Supabase), migrations, limits (PDF ~4.5MB on Hobby).
- **`README.md`** — Local quick start + links to free AI keys.
- **`package.json`** — `vercel-build` script if you want **`prisma migrate deploy`** on build (optional; needs `DATABASE_URL` at build time).
- **`next.config.mjs`** — `serverActions.bodySizeLimit: '15mb'` for larger uploads where the platform allows it.

### AI: **free only** (Anthropic removed)

- **`lib/ai.ts`** uses **`fetch`** only:
  1. **Groq** — `GROQ_API_KEY` from [https://console.groq.com/keys](https://console.groq.com/keys) (primary).
  2. **Google Gemini** — `GEMINI_API_KEY` from [https://aistudio.google.com/apikey](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey) if Groq fails or isn’t set.

Optional env: `GROQ_MODEL` (default `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`), `GEMINI_MODEL` (default `gemini-2.0-flash`).

`.env.example` is updated accordingly.

### Tests: **Give test** + **Create test**

- **`app/test/page.tsx`** — Tabs: **Give test** | **Create test**.
- **Give test** — Loads **`GET /api/test/sample`**, timer, MCQ flow, score %, retry.
- **Create test** — PDF upload → **`POST /api/test/upload-pdf`** (validates PDF, records stub metadata when DB exists; JSON response explains next pipeline steps).
- **`SidebarNav`** — **Test** link enabled (no longer “Soon”).
- **Prisma** — **`TestPdfUpload`** model for upload skeleton rows.

### Deploy command (`/deploy`)

This environment **does not return usable terminal output** for `vercel`, so I **cannot** confirm CLI presence, link state, or completion URLs from here. Run this **on your machine** from the repo:

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
# Preflight: npm i -g vercel   if needed
# If not linked: vercel link
vercel          # preview (default)
# Production only after you explicitly want it:
# vercel --prod
```

Then:

```bash
vercel inspect <deployment-url>
```

**Git:** If `git status` is dirty, uncommitted files **won’t** be what Vercel builds unless you push to a connected Git integration—commit/push first if you use GitHub.

**Observability (warnings, not blockers):** On Pro/Enterprise, configure **drains** or another error sink if you want production errors forwarded; `@vercel/analytics` is already in the app for basic analytics.
